question_title: Will the U.S. Senate confirm at least five federal Article III judicial nominees before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether 5+ Article III judicial nominees will be confirmed from March 10 through April 30, 2026. As of March 28, only 1 has been confirmed (Anna St. John, March 17). Only 3 more Article III nominees sit on the Senate Executive Calendar: Shepherd and Wolfe (both with cloture filed, votes expected April 13) and Davis (on calendar, no cloture yet). This creates a hard pipeline ceiling of 4 from the current calendar. For the 5th, the Judiciary Committee would need to advance one of the March 2 nominees (Smith, Kuhlman, Clarke, etc.) through hearing, committee vote, and floor confirmation in the ~12 business days available (April 13-30), while the Senate is also dealing with other priorities. The most likely outcome is 4 confirmations (1 done + 3 calendar nominees), just short of the threshold. Reaching 5 requires favorable timing on committee action.

factors.description: Only 1 Article III nominee confirmed since March 10 (Anna St. John on March 17), leaving 4 more needed in remaining time
Only 3 Article III nominees on Senate Executive Calendar (Davis, Shepherd, Wolfe) - maximum of 4 total from current pipeline, falling short of the 5-judge threshold
Cloture filed on Shepherd and Wolfe, with Senate scheduled to vote on cloture April 13 - these 2 confirmations are near-certain
Senate in recess March 27 through April 12 (pro forma only), reducing available business days to approximately 12 (April 13-30)
Several nominees received March 2 are still in committee (Smith, Kuhlman, Mattivi, Clarke, Westercamp, Powell) - could potentially be advanced and confirmed by April 30 if committee acts quickly
factors.conditional: If Judiciary Committee schedules markup for March 2 nominees during April session

factors.description: February 2026 pace was 6 confirmations in one month, demonstrating Senate can move quickly on judicial nominees when motivated
Republican majority controls both committee and floor schedule, and judicial confirmations are stated administration priority

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.25 (25%)**

The key constraint is the **pipeline**: only 3 Article III judicial nominees are on the Senate Executive Calendar (Shepherd, Wolfe, Davis), and only 1 (St. John) has been confirmed since March 10. Even if all 3 calendar nominees are confirmed, that yields just 4 — one short of the threshold.

For 5, the Judiciary Committee would need to advance at least one of the March 2 nominees through hearing, committee vote, and full Senate confirmation in the ~12 remaining business days (April 13-30). The Senate returns April 13 with cloture votes on Shepherd and Wolfe already scheduled, making those near-certain. Davis (on calendar but no cloture filed) is likely but not guaranteed. The 5th nominee requires everything to break favorably on the committee timeline — possible but not probable.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if five or more federal Article III judicial nominees are confirmed by the U.S. Senate from March 10, 2026 through April 30, 2026. It resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is the Senate Judiciary Committee judicial nominations page at https://www.judiciary.senate.gov/nominations/judicial
fine_print: Count only confirmations on or after March 10, 2026. Include Supreme Court, circuit court, district court, and Court of International Trade nominees, but exclude territorial judges and non-Article III judicial offices. Candidate Sources: U.S. Senate Executive Calendar PDF https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/executive_calendar/xcalv.pdf ; Senate Judiciary Committee homepage https://www.judiciary.senate.gov/
